practice_boundaries.detail: Skill explicitly forbids recommending patient care or clinical decisions

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 Evidence Insight, text analysis only

reliability.note: Handles vague inputs via explicit assumptions, but no structured minimum-clarification protocol before full output

performance_context.note: 329 lines; seven reference modules properly separate depth from main body

agent_usability.note: Step 2 literature retrieval instruction is detailed but may cause the model to attempt real-time web retrieval when not available

agent_specific.note: Description is rich with trigger phrases; Step 2 literature retrieval dependency may cause inconsistent outputs without web access

inputs.label: Ferroptosis pathway finding in pancreatic cancer — translational opportunity scan

assertions.text: Discovery unit explicitly defined before opportunity mapping
At least two plausible translational paths compared side by side
Bridge evidence strength explicitly rated for each path
No fabricated clinical trial data or validation status presented
One primary recommended next-step direction given with evidence rationale

inputs.label: Macrophage polarization signature in lupus — clinical interface mapping

assertions.text: Mechanism relevance separated from translational usability
Feasibility and burden analysis included for each path
Crowded or poorly differentiated directions identified
Self-critical risk review present
Animal-only evidence not presented as sufficient translational proof

inputs.label: Endothelial dysfunction pathway in sepsis — therapeutic and biomarker paths

assertions.text: Barrier and failure-point table present for each path
Assay detectability and sample access burden assessed
Premature and crowded translational paths flagged
Priority opportunity summary identifies best immediate vs high-upside paths
No patient-specific clinical recommendations made

inputs.label: Single-cell immune exhaustion finding in chronic infection with weak human bridge evidence

assertions.text: Weak human bridge evidence explicitly labeled rather than implied as sufficient
Mechanism-first framing recommended over direct translational framing when evidence is thin
Translation barriers table produced
No overclaimed therapeutic relevance from model-only data
Most important missing evidence link identified

inputs.label: Multiple mechanism findings — comparative priority ranking across five translational paths

assertions.text: All five paths compared with bridge-evidence and burden tables
Paths not worth prioritizing explicitly identified
Recommended path justified as superior to alternatives
No fabricated precedents or validation status used to support rankings
Self-critical risk review identifies most assumption-dependent ranking decision

key_strengths: Strong separation of mechanism relevance from translational usability — core analytical discipline enforced throughout
Mandatory barrier and failure-point analysis prevents rose-tinted opportunity framing
Seven reference modules covering discovery units, clinical interfaces, feasibility, and translation barriers provide comprehensive coverage
Self-critical risk review required in Step 8 ensures honest evaluation of recommendation strength

recommendations.title: Step 2 literature retrieval creates inconsistent behavior without web access
recommendations.problem: Step 2 instructs retrieval of peer-reviewed literature before framing, but without web access the model may silently skip this step or produce unreliable field memory as if it were retrieved evidence.
recommendations.root_cause: The retrieval instruction assumes live search capability without specifying a fallback behavior for offline or context-only execution.
recommendations.fix: Add a retrieval-mode fallback: if live search is unavailable, explicitly instruct the model to label all evidence claims as 'based on training knowledge, not live retrieval' and prompt the user to provide key papers if available.

recommendations.title: No clarification protocol for under-specified discovery inputs
recommendations.problem: When a discovery is described only at the field level (e.g., 'ferroptosis in cancer'), the skill proceeds with assumptions rather than asking 2–3 focused questions to narrow the discovery unit.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section describes valid inputs but does not define a minimum clarification protocol.
recommendations.fix: Add a Clarification Block after Input Validation: if disease context, discovery type, or translational use-case preference is absent, ask 2–3 targeted questions before full mapping.
